title: GitOps & Deployments
description: GitOps uses Git as the single source of truth for declarative infrastructure and application deployments, with automated reconciliation loops.

sections.title: GitOps Principles
sections.description: GitOps has four core principles: declarative configuration stored in Git, desired state reconciled automatically, idempotent and deterministic operations, and continuous observation of actual state. Systems like ArgoCD and Flux CD continuously compare the Git state with cluster state and correct drift. The operator watches the repo, diffs manifests, and applies changes to reach the desired state.

sections.title: Terraform + Actions
sections.description:
Combine Terraform with GitHub Actions for infrastructure provisioning. Use the hashicorp/setup-terraform action with a remote backend (S3, AzureRM, GCS). Store state remotely with locking. Run terraform plan in PRs with tfsec for security scanning, and terraform apply on merge to main. Use OIDC to authenticate Terraform to cloud providers without static credentials.

jobs:
  terraform:
    permissions:
      id-token: write
      contents: read
    steps:
      - uses: hashicorp/setup-terraform@v3
      - run: terraform plan -out=tfplan

sections.title: OIDC Auth
sections.description:
OpenID Connect replaces long-lived cloud secrets with short-lived tokens. GitHub Actions requests an OIDC token from https://token.actions.githubusercontent.com. The cloud provider (AWS, Azure, GCP) validates the token against a configured trust policy. Claims include sub (repo owner/repo), ref (branch/tag), and actor (username).

# AWS trust policy condition
"Condition": {
  "StringLike": {
    "token.actions.githubusercontent.com:sub": "repo:org/repo:*"
  }
}

sections.title: ArgoCD Integration
sections.description:
ArgoCD integrates with GitHub via the argocd-image-updater and webhook triggers. Configure a webhook from GitHub to ArgoCD to trigger sync on push. Use ApplicationSets for multi-environment deployments with generators for Git file, cluster, or pull request generation. Enable auto-sync with selfHeal and prune for drift correction.

apiVersion: argoproj.io/v1alpha1
kind: Application
metadata:
  name: my-app
spec:
  source:
    repoURL: https://github.com/org/repo.git
    path: k8s/overlays/prod
  syncPolicy:
    automated:
      prune: true
      selfHeal: true

sections.title: Environment Promotion
sections.description:
Promote deployments through dev -> staging -> canary -> prod using Git merges. Each environment has a branch or directory (k8s/overlays/dev, k8s/overlays/prod). Use PRs for environment promotion with required approvals. Deployment frequency metrics track lead time from merge to production sync. GitHub Actions environment blocks tie deployment approvals to environments.

jobs:
  deploy-prod:
    environment: production
    steps:
      - run: kubectl apply -f k8s/overlays/prod

details: GitOps shifts from imperative (run command) to declarative (describe desired state) operations. The reconciliation loop is the core abstraction: the operator (ArgoCD/Flux) polls or watches the Git repository for changes, diffs the desired state against the cluster/cloud state, and applies the diff. Any manual change to the cluster is overwritten on the next sync (drift correction). OIDC authentication is critical for security — GitHub Actions receives an OIDC token (JWT) from the GitHub OIDC provider, which includes claims like repository, ref, sha, and workflow. The cloud provider's trust policy validates these claims, returning a cloud access token that is short-lived (typically 1 hour). No secrets to store in GitHub Secrets. For multi-environment GitOps, structure repos with kustomize overlays or Helm value files per environment. Use ApplicationSets in ArgoCD with the Git generator to automatically create Applications for each directory or branch. Environment promotion should follow your release strategy: blue-green or canary via Flagger or Argo Rollouts. Monitor deployment events via the GitHub Deployments API — each deployment creates a deployment event visible in the UI with environment, commit, and status.
cheatSheet:
Core Commands

  • kubectl apply -k k8s/overlays/prod — apply Kustomize overlay from Git.
  • argocd app sync my-app — trigger ArgoCD sync from CLI.
  • terraform plan -out=tfplan — plan infrastructure changes in PR.

Staff+ Patterns

  • OIDC between GitHub Actions and cloud providers replaces all static secrets — id-token: write permission, cloud trust policy validates claims.
  • ArgoCD ApplicationSets with Git generator auto-create Apps for each directory or branch.
  • Environment promotion via Git merges: dev branch → staging branch → prod tag. Each promotion runs a PR with approvals.

Watch Outs

  • Drift correction (auto-sync with selfHeal) can overwrite emergency manual fixes — use sparingly.
  • Terraform state locking is critical in CI — without it, concurrent applies corrupt state.
  • OIDC token claims must be specific — repo:org/repo:* is too broad. Scope to ref:refs/heads/main.

Interview Angle

Netflix/Amazon L6: "Design a GitOps deployment pipeline for 200 microservices." Discuss: environment repos, promotion gates, canary analysis (Flagger/Argo Rollouts), and automated rollback via git revert with the deployment tag as the rollback target.
